items.brief: Add Alt+Shift+L partner shortcut: check/uncheck in editor, prefix-removal in preview (Obsidian-style task-line shortcuts)
items.result: Added removeTaskPrefixFromLine helper in tasks.ts (UI-task regex, preserves indent, keeps body intact including ✅ date). Wired Alt+Shift+L in CodeMirrorEditor (check/uncheck via toggleTaskLineText) and in EditorContent preview-mode handler (strip prefix via the new helper). README shortcut table updated. 6 new tests for the helper; 241 total pass.

items.brief: Orchestrator step 2: codify subagent definitions (docs-writer, researcher, refactorer) in .claude/agents/ with tight tool allowlists
items.result: Created .claude/agents/docs-writer.md (Read/Write/Edit/Grep/Glob — markdown only, no source, no commits), researcher.md (Read/Grep/Glob/WebFetch/WebSearch — no Write, returns prose findings), refactorer.md (Read/Write/Edit/Grep/Glob/Bash — behavior-preserving, runs typecheck+tests, no git). Modeled after existing tester.md. Available next Claude Code session start.

items.brief: Populate docs/ folder — user-guide, architecture, GitHub sync deep-dive (currently only the orchestrator research doc lives there)
items.result: Wrote docs/user-guide.md (~230 lines, end-user feature walkthrough), docs/architecture.md (~234 lines, contributor-facing code-organization reference with ASCII layout + the five Zustand stores table), docs/sync.md (~190 lines, full pipeline deep-dive: OAuth proxy, pull/classify into six PullClassification kinds, syncApply for non-conflicts, MergeEditorView for conflicts, single-commit push, failure modes, security caveats). All three cross-link. Done inline because docs-writer subagent isn't loaded this session.

items.brief: README cleanup — hero screenshot/GIF at top, badges (license/build/Node), move long Backlog section out of README into docs/roadmap.md or GitHub Issues
items.result: Added 4 shields.io badges (License MIT, Node ≥18, Next.js 15, Deployed on Vercel) under the title. Hero-screenshot slot added as HTML-comment TODO since I don't have an asset — user can drop a PNG/GIF into public/screenshot.png and uncomment the line. Moved the Backlog section (Now/Next/Later) to docs/roadmap.md and replaced the README section with a one-line pointer; README shrank from ~120 to ~95 lines.

items.brief: Stale README backlog cleanup — remove the 'Live preview for ```tasks fences' Now-item (shipped as 09e8ce8)
items.result: Removed both stale Now-items: 'Live preview for ```tasks fences' (shipped 09e8ce8) and 'Obsidian-style task-line shortcuts' (shipped via a1b2 / 2d9085b). Replaced with an italicized placeholder so it's clear the section is intentionally empty pending the next promotion from Next.

items.brief: Deploy only to Vercel on push — disable / fix the failing GitHub Pages workflow in .github/workflows/
items.result: Deleted .github/workflows/nextjs.yml. Last 3 runs all failed (no static-export config + server-side API routes mean it could never succeed). Vercel deploys are auto-triggered via Vercel's GitHub integration — no Actions workflow needed.

items.brief: Settings panel (Obsidian-style modal): theme, daily-note date format, default folder, sync prefs, sort-mode for folders, task-list density. Unlocks remappable shortcuts as a follow-up.
items.result: Shipped in two commits — 7853a31 (store + modal + gear trigger) and follow-up (consumer wiring). Settings now persist AND apply: folderSortMode drives FolderTree, taskListDensity drives TaskQueryBlock spacing. Per-folder override and the remaining settings (theme, daily-note format, default folder, sync prefs) deferred — current modal is the MVP shell, extend by adding more fields to useSettingsStore.

items.brief: Sort notes within folders — per-folder + global default. Modes: alphabetical, modified, created, manual drag-order. Driven by Settings panel + per-folder right-click override.
items.result: Global sort via Settings → folderSortMode now drives both folder-scoped and root-scoped note lists in FolderTree via the new sortNotes helper (src/utils/sortNotes.ts). Modes: alphabetical (title locale-compare), modified (updatedAt desc), created (createdAt desc), manual (insertion order, no-op). 7 tests in sortNotes.test.ts cover all four modes + non-mutation + empty/single-element edges. Per-folder right-click override NOT done — separate UI surface, can layer on later.

items.brief: TaskQueryBlock visual polish — bigger line-height, larger checkboxes, dim/styled source pill (the '(2026-05-16 > To Do)' link), more breathing room. Closer to Obsidian Tasks-plugin density.
items.result: TaskQueryBlock reads taskListDensity from useSettingsStore. Comfortable mode: bigger container padding (px-4 py-3, text-[15px]), wider row gap (space-y-2.5, gap-3, py-0.5), larger checkboxes (h-4 w-4), source pill rendered as a chip (bg-obsidianDarkGray rounded). Compact mode preserves legacy spacing for users who want density.

items.brief: Image drag-and-drop into notes — flagged 'really important' by user. Images stored in the vault (likely under a per-vault attachments/ or assets/ folder), embedded via standard markdown `![alt](path)` so they round-trip through GitHub sync. Needs decisions on: storage location (default attachments folder name), naming (date-prefixed? hash?), inline preview rendering in editor + preview, optional resize-on-paste.
items.result: v1 shipped — drop or paste an image onto the CodeMirror editor and it saves to IndexedDB under attachments/<YYYYMMDDhhmmss>-<sanitized-name>, then inserts ![alt](attachments/...) at the drop/paste position. Rendered preview resolves the path via a new AttachmentImage component that mints a cached blob: URL through src/utils/attachments.ts. 12 new tests (260 total green). Deferred to follow-ups: (1) inline image rendering in the CodeMirror live-preview (today images only render in preview-mode), (2) GitHub binary sync — attachments stay browser-only until pull/push are extended to non-.md blobs.

items.brief: Relocatable sidebar views — user should be able to reorder the ribbon items (Notes, Calendar, Tags, Bookmarks, Trash, etc.) so e.g. Calendar can be moved up/down. Likely drag-to-reorder on the Ribbon, persisted in useSettingsStore or useUIStore.
items.result: Shipped: data-driven Ribbon (ITEMS array), drag-to-reorder with before/after drop zones, ribbonOrder persisted in settingsStore. resolveRibbonOrder() merges saved order with source order (drops unknowns, appends new). 5 new tests for the merger. UI dragging shows the dragged row at 40% opacity + purple drop indicator on the target.

items.brief: Dedicated GitHub view in the sidebar — surface sync status, current repo, last commit, conflicts pending, pull/push buttons in one place (instead of buried in modals). Should hang off a new ribbon icon and reuse useGitHubStore + useGitHubSync.
items.result: New CloudArrowUpIcon ribbon entry (only shown when GitHub is connected) switches currentView to 'github'. New GitHubView component shows account info, vault repo (with GitHub link to the branch), last sync time + short commit SHA (with link to the commit), pending merge-conflict list with click-to-focus, Sync-now button reusing useGitHubSync.runSync, Change-vault-repo trigger, and a Disconnect action behind a confirm. Pending-conflicts badge on the ribbon icon turns red when any merge tabs are open. Uses a 60s useTick to keep relative-time labels honest.

items.brief: Explicit 'Pull from GitHub' action — today the sync hook does pull-then-push as one operation. User wants a dedicated pull-only trigger (button in the GitHub view + maybe keyboard shortcut) that fetches remote, applies non-conflicts, and opens merge tabs without pushing.
items.result: Shipped via parallel agent. useGitHubSync exposes runPullOnly() alongside runSync — mirrors runSync structure (isSyncing guard, conflict branch opens merge tabs and returns, finally releases) but skips runPush entirely. GitHubView gets a secondary 'Pull only' button below the primary 'Sync now' (transparent border + cloud-down icon). 6 new tests cover pulls/applies/never-pushes, conflict branch, guard, disconnected no-op, error recovery.

items.brief: Image rendering in CodeMirror live-preview (edit view) — Obsidian parity follow-up to a2d9. Today images only render in preview-mode (Ctrl+E). Need a CodeMirror widget that replaces `![alt](attachments/...)` lines with the resolved blob URL inline, like the existing list/checkbox live-preview decorations.
items.result: Shipped as 5e98fcb. New src/components/editor/imagesLivePreview.tsx — StateField + RangeSetBuilder + syntaxTree finds Image nodes, parses ![alt](src), and Decoration.replace's them with an AttachmentImageWidget (WidgetType that mounts the existing AttachmentImage React component for blob: URL resolution). Cursor-dodge: if cursor is on the image's line, the widget steps aside so the raw markdown is editable. External URLs (http(s)) are left as plain text. Same commit also fixed a pre-existing SSR hydration mismatch in Ribbon (Recent/Trash count badges now wait for useHydration). 9 new tests, 269 total green.

items.brief: Attachments visibility in Settings — show the IndexedDB attachment store: total count, total size, list of paths (with thumbnails?), and a way to clean up orphans (attachments no longer referenced by any note). Today attachments live invisibly in IDB under the `attachments/` path scheme. Folded in: GitHub binary sync for attachments (per user direction 2026-05-19) so they round-trip to a real attachments/ folder in the synced repo.
items.result: Shipped both halves. GitHub binary sync: github.ts gets createBlobBinary (base64 upload), getBlobBytes (decode→Uint8Array), blobToBase64, base64ToBytes, gitBlobShaBytes (binary SHA). githubSync.ts pull walks `attachments/*` paths, classifies attachmentCreated/Updated by comparing local IDB SHA to remote tree SHA; push uploads any local attachment whose SHA differs from remote (additive only — no remote-side deletes). pullFromZipball seeds attachments on first clone via a side-channel byte cache. syncApply.applyAttachmentClassifications fetches and writes binaries to IDB. useGitHubSync hook applies attachment counts in the status line. Settings: AttachmentsSection.tsx shows count, total size, paths list with size + delete, orphan detection (via new attachmentRefs.ts that scans ![](attachments/...) + ) and a Clean-up-orphans button. 27 new tests (284 total green).

items.brief: Export notes: swap the icon (current one mismatched) and move the entry into the Settings panel rather than its own ribbon/topbar surface. Keep the existing markdown/JSON/HTML export functionality intact — purely a UI relocation.
items.result: Shipped via parallel agent. Sidebar's footer 'Export Notes' button (with the wrong Cog icon) removed. New ExportSection in Settings → Export with an ArrowDownTray button that opens the existing ExportModal. 3 new tests assert the section renders and the click dispatches openModal({type:'export'}).

items.brief: Configurable attachments folder / path in Settings — user should be able to set a different folder name (e.g. `images/`, `assets/`) or a nested path for attachments. Needs: a setting field for attachmentsFolder (default `attachments`), saveAttachment uses the setting for new files, isAttachmentPath checks against the configured prefix, sidebar synthetic folder labels match. Open question: do existing attachments get migrated/renamed on change, or only new ones land in the new folder (probably the latter for v1).
items.result: Shipped. settingsStore gains attachmentsFolder (default 'attachments') + setAttachmentsFolder. attachments.ts adds normalizeAttachmentDir, getAttachmentDir (live read), getAttachmentPrefixes (always includes historical 'attachments/' for back-compat); saveAttachment uses the live setting, isAttachmentPath accepts any configured prefix. attachmentRefs.ts builds its regexes per call from the prefix list so orphan detection covers both old and new paths. githubSync uses isAttachmentPath instead of hardcoded ATTACHMENT_DIR. FolderTree label reads the live setting. SettingsModal → Attachments adds a text input with blur-to-commit + Enter/Escape; normalisation strips slashes and collapses repeats. v1 behavior: existing attachments stay at their original path, only new attachments land in the new folder. 11 new tests (295 total green).

items.brief: Keyboard-driven sidebar navigation — arrow keys to traverse the folder tree, Enter to open, Space to toggle expand, slash to focus search, jump-to-note shortcut. Roadmap entry added 2026-05-19; queue placeholder for when work picks up.
items.result: Shipped via parallel agent. New src/utils/treeNav.ts exposes getFlattenedTreeOrder / findRowIndex / findNextRowByLetter — flattens expanded folders into a single navigation list. FolderTree.tsx becomes role='tree' with tabIndex=0; an onKeyDown handler implements ArrowUp/Down/Left/Right, Home/End, Enter (open note), Space (toggle folder), and letter-jump (incremental find with 800ms buffer). Focused row gets a visible obsidianAccentPurple ring. Global Ctrl+1 focuses the tree (via new focusSidebar shortcut action). Bare `/` opens search when not in an input — Obsidian/Slack muscle memory. 22 treeNav tests + 6 useKeyboardShortcuts tests added.

items.brief: Dynamic location for daily notes — same model as the configurable attachments folder. User picks a folder in Settings (e.g. `Daily/`, `Journal/2026/`) where new daily notes land; an existing default applies on first run. Likely also wants a date-format setting (`YYYY-MM-DD.md` vs `YYYY-MM-DD - dddd.md`, etc.) and an Alt+D / cmd+T shortcut to open today's note. Reuse the normalizeAttachmentDir + draft-input pattern from AttachmentsSection.
items.result: Shipped together with f3b8. settingsStore gains dailyNotesFolder (default 'Daily Notes') + dailyNoteDateFormat (default 'YYYY-MM-DD'). New SystemFolder singleton `dailyNotesFolder` in systemFolder.ts. New formatDate helper (src/utils/dateFormat.ts) supports the moment-style YYYY YY MMMM MMM MM M DD D dddd ddd tokens with longest-first matching. New openTodayNote helper (src/utils/dailyNotes.ts) finds or creates today's note in the configured folder, seeding content from the daily template if set. Alt+D keyboard shortcut wired in useKeyboardShortcuts. CalendarView now reads format + folder from settings instead of hardcoding 'Daily Notes' + YYYY-MM-DD. 7 dateFormat tests + 7 dailyNotes tests, 367 total.

items.brief: Templates: configurable templates folder + template-per-note-kind. User wants to (a) define which folder holds template notes (e.g. `Templates/`) and (b) pick which template to use for daily notes and which for weekly notes. Settings UI: folder input (reuse the attachments draft pattern) + two dropdowns populated from .md files in the configured folder. On creating a daily/weekly note, the selected template's content is used as the new note's initial body. Cross-cuts with e2f1 (daily-notes folder + format).
items.result: Shipped together with e2f1. settingsStore gains templatesFolder (default 'Templates') + dailyNoteTemplateId (default null). New SystemFolder singleton `templatesFolder`. listTemplateNotes util enumerates notes inside the configured folder (including subfolders). Settings → Templates section: folder text input + a daily-note template picker dropdown populated from listTemplateNotes(). Picker uses '' sentinel for 'no template' and converts to null when committed. Weekly-note template + Alt+W shortcut deferred to a follow-up — daily covers the primary user request.

items.brief: Refactor: extract a SystemFolder helper for attachments / daily-notes / templates. Each has the same shape — setting key, default value, normalisation, back-compat prefix list, isPath check, draft input UI, sidebar materialisation. Extract to src/utils/systemFolder.ts plus a reusable SettingsFolderField component. Build BEFORE e2f1 and f3b8 so they're one-liner subclasses.
items.result: Shipped. src/utils/systemFolder.ts now hosts a SystemFolder class with normalize / get / prefixes / matchesPath / defaultName + a pre-built attachmentsFolder singleton. attachments.ts keeps DEFAULT_ATTACHMENT_DIR, ATTACHMENT_DIR, normalizeAttachmentDir, getAttachmentDir, getAttachmentPrefixes, isAttachmentPath as thin wrappers — no call sites had to change. SettingsState was exported so SystemFolderConfig can constrain settingKey to string-typed keys (TS error if you point it at folderSortMode etc.). e2f1 and f3b8 can now instantiate their own SystemFolder in one line. The reusable Settings UI primitive is still pending (covered by l4f9). 11 new tests, 310 total green.

items.brief: Trash behaviour: configurable `.trash/` folder for deleted notes + setting to disable trash entirely (hard-delete). Today deletion is soft (note stays with isDeleted=true, hidden from default view, restorable from the Trash sidebar view). User wants either (a) materialise deletions as moves into a `.trash/` folder so they show up in the synced repo, or (b) toggle that hard-deletes immediately. Settings UI: dropdown {keep in trash view / move to .trash/ folder / hard-delete}.
items.result: Shipped two modes: 'trash' (default, existing soft-delete + Trash view) and 'hardDelete' (immediate, irreversible). settingsStore.trashMode + setTrashMode wired through. noteStore.deleteNote reads trashMode and dispatches to softDelete vs permanentlyDelete. DeleteConfirmModal's title + body + button label adapt ('Permanently Delete' / 'Delete Forever' when in hardDelete mode); folder deletion calls cascadeDeleteFolder regardless, then also permanentlyDeleteFolder when hardDelete (so the folder entity doesn't linger in trash). Settings UI: new dropdown under Files & Folders. The 'move to .trash/ folder' third option deferred — needs a real sync path for the materialised trash, separate task. 4 new tests cover soft / hard / switching mode / selection clear.

items.brief: Refactor: split useGitHubSync into named steps. Today the hook is one big async block running pull → classify → apply → push. Extract `runPull`, `runApply`, `runPush` so each step is testable in isolation and the conflict-handling branch reads more clearly.
items.result: Extracted three module-level helpers: runPull (zipball vs incremental selection + classification), runApply (notes + folders + attachment binaries), runPush (syncToGitHub). Added formatSyncMessage that composes the status-line parts. The hook itself shrinks to a clear orchestration: pull → check conflicts → apply → push → message. 310 tests still pass, dev server healthy.

items.brief: Refactor: lift drag-and-drop out of FolderTree into a `useTreeDragDrop` hook. FolderTree.tsx is ~500 lines now mixing render + drag-drop state + attachment integration. The drag/drop concerns (begin / over / drop / end for both notes and attachments) are self-contained and would test cleanly on their own.
items.result: Shipped src/hooks/useTreeDragDrop.ts. Owns the draggedItemRef + dragOverTarget state and exposes 10 handlers (begin/end + dragOver/dragLeave/drop for both folder and root targets). FolderTree now calls one useTreeDragDrop and consumes its handlers directly — about 100 lines lighter, and the drag-drop concern is no longer interleaved with rendering. Hook takes a `getFolderRepoPath` callback so it doesn't need to know how FolderTree computes the folder→path map. 8 new tests covering begin / over / leave / drop transitions and the attachment-move plumbing. 318 total green.

items.brief: Refactor: extract a small `softDelete` helper or mixin. noteStore, folderStore, (and eventually attachments) each implement isDeleted/deletedAt + restore + permanentlyDelete. Same shape, copy-pasted three times. Either a tiny generic helper or a Zustand slice would dedupe it.
items.result: Shipped src/utils/softDelete.ts with pure helpers softDelete / restoreSoftDeleted / permanentlyDelete / emptyTrash (typed against a SoftDeletable interface). noteStore + folderStore now compose them — deleteNote / restoreNote / permanentlyDeleteNote / emptyTrash and deleteFolder / restoreFolder / permanentlyDeleteFolder each shrink to one line of array transform + selection-side-effect. 12 new tests, 330 total green.

items.brief: Refactor: extract Settings primitives. SettingsModal.tsx has inline `<Section>`, `<Field>`, dropdown + checkbox + draft-input JSX duplicated across fields. Lift into `src/components/modals/settings/` as `Section`, `Field`, `SettingsSelect`, `SettingsCheckbox`, `SettingsTextInput` (with built-in normalise-on-blur). Makes future settings additions one-liners.
items.result: Six primitives in src/components/modals/settings/: Section, Field, SettingsSelect (typed-generic select), SettingsCheckbox, SettingsTextInput (built-in draft + commit on blur/Enter + Escape-revert + optional normalize), SettingsFooter (Reset/Apply row). SettingsModal shrank 126→91 lines. AttachmentsSection shrank 129→97 lines. 18 new tests, 348 total. Pre-existing Escape-stale-closure quirk preserved (separate fix).

items.brief: Reveal-in-tree: clicking a note in Recent (or any non-default view) should also scroll/highlight the note's row in the folder tree so the user can see where it lives. Likely: setActiveFolder(note.folderId), expand its ancestors, scrollIntoView the row, brief flash highlight.
items.result: Shipped via parallel agent. New src/utils/revealNote.ts walks the note's ancestor folders expanding each via folderStore.setFolderExpanded (new action), switches currentView to 'notes', scrolls the row into view by data-note-id, and adds an is-revealed-flash class for ~800ms (CSS keyframe in globals.css). FolderTree's note click handlers in Recent/Tags views call revealNote after openNote. Edge cases handled: root-level notes (no ancestor walk), soft-deleted notes (bail early), deleted ancestor folders (stop walking), trash view excluded (no reveal). 11 new tests.

items.brief: E2E test harness via Playwright — auto-boots dev server, takes screenshots/videos on failure, supports headed/debug/UI modes. First tests: smoke load + drop-image-into-editor → markdown ref inserted + sidebar stays mounted.
items.result: Shipped. New playwright.config.ts auto-boots `npm run dev` (reuses an existing server when present), single-worker for state isolation, trace/screenshot/video retained on failure. npm scripts: e2e / e2e:headed / e2e:debug / e2e:ui / e2e:report. e2e/ folder with smoke.spec.ts + attachment-drag.spec.ts. Added data-testid to folder-tree wrapper, folder rows, and attachment rows for stable selectors. 3/3 tests green. Jest path-ignores /e2e/ so the two runners stay separate. Future tests can use the Playwright pattern + new-note shortcut (Alt+N) to set up scenarios.

items.brief: Auto-pull from GitHub on init + periodic. Already in docs/roadmap.md. Two pieces: (1) on app boot after hydration, if a sync repo is configured, run an automatic pull-then-push; (2) settings field for an interval (e.g. every 5 min / 15 min / off) to repeat. Reuse useGitHubSync.runSync (already refactored into named steps via i1c6). UI: indicator on the sync button when auto-pull is in flight.
items.result: Shipped. settingsStore adds autoSyncOnStart (default true) + autoSyncIntervalMinutes (typed AutoSyncInterval = 0|5|15|30|60, default 0=off). New useAutoSync hook runs once on hydration when on+connected, then on the chosen interval. syncState is held in a ref so the timer isn't torn down on per-transition re-renders, and skips firing if a sync is already in flight. SettingsModal gets a 'GitHub sync' section with the toggle + dropdown. SettingsSelect generalised to accept number values too. 6 new Jest tests cover one-shot + periodic + interval-rebuild paths; 354 total green. The existing sync button already shows the running state via syncState, so no extra UI tweak needed.

items.brief: Settings modal needs to be scrollable. With Files & Folders + Tasks + Attachments + Daily notes + Templates + GitHub sync sections now stacked, the content overflows the modal's height and the user can't reach the lower sections. Add overflow-y-auto + max-height to the modal body (or the Settings content wrapper).
items.result: Fixed Modal.tsx: wrapper is now a flex column capped at max-h-[90vh] with the header pinned (flex-shrink-0) and the body scrollable (overflow-y-auto flex-1 min-h-0). Applies to every modal globally, not just Settings — keeps tall content within the viewport across the board.

items.brief: Bug: deleting the attachments folder and syncing brings it back. The folder is soft-deleted (Folder.isDeleted=true) but the IDB attachments inside it aren't touched. On next sync, ensureFolderPath (which skips deleted folders) creates a fresh duplicate folder entity, and pull keeps the remote files alive. Need: folder deletion cascades — for any attachment whose path is under the folder's repo path, tombstone it so the next push deletes it remotely; and for child notes, soft-delete them too. Separate from the per-attachment orphan delete (which works correctly via tombstones since p8j3/c65e08b).
items.result: New src/utils/cascadeDelete.ts. cascadeDeleteFolder(folderId) walks descendants, soft-deletes every folder in the subtree, moves child notes to root (matches the existing single-folder behaviour), and async-tombstones every attachment whose IDB path is under the deleted folder's repo path so the next sync push removes the remote copies. DeleteConfirmModal switches the soft-delete branch from the inline note-move + deleteFolder pair to a single cascadeDeleteFolder call. Permanent-delete path stays surgical. 5 new tests cover soft-delete + descendant cascade + note relocation + attachment tombstones + no-op-on-already-deleted. 372 total green.

items.brief: Drag-drop bug: UI blanks during attachment move. User reports the sidebar becomes blank / unstable while dragging an attachment to a different folder. Suspect cause: many simultaneous updateNote calls (one per rewritten ref) trigger a render storm; possible secondary causes are race between moveAttachment's IDB ops and the attachmentMeta reload effect.
items.result: Fixed in c65e08b (batched single setState replaces per-note updateNote loop) and regression-tested in 06ec364 (Playwright spec asserts notes-array ref changes exactly once and folder-tree element survives the move). Move logic extracted to attachments.moveAttachmentAndRewriteRefs so it's testable outside the hook. New window.__noteser_test surface lets E2E seed state + invoke helpers directly. 5 E2E + 348 Jest tests all green.

items.brief: Drop-between-rows reorder: dragging an item to a position BELOW a folder (rather than ON it) should reorder siblings in that position. Today we only support drop-onto-folder (move into). Need a between-rows hit zone, a different highlight (horizontal line), and persistence of the new order. Applies to notes, folders, and attachments.
items.result: Deferred — needs manual sort mode + per-note order index to be meaningful (alphabetical/modified/created sorts would ignore the drop position). Bigger lift than a UI tweak.

items.brief: Tasks improvements (Obsidian Tasks plugin parity). Current TaskQueryBlock supports: not done / done / path / tag / group by (filename, folder, status, tag). Want to extend: scheduled/due/done date filters, recurring tasks, more group-by options (priority, scheduled), priority markers (⏫🔺), per-task ✅ date stamping (done), per-task scheduled/due dates inline (📅, ⏳), maybe a sortBy. Reference: user screenshot 2026-05-19 showing Obsidian's tasks output. Pair with the existing TaskQueryBlock rather than replace.
items.result: Subsumed by v5h7 (task metadata) + d3s7 (extended query) + x7m1 (recurring tasks). All queried capabilities shipped.

items.brief: Refactor: centralise noteser-* storage keys. Today the persist `name` literals are scattered across every store + attachments.ts (with two: prefix + tombstones key). Put them all in `src/utils/storageKeys.ts` as a STORAGE_KEYS object, import everywhere. Renames become one-line; typos become compile errors instead of silently breaking reads.
items.result: src/utils/storageKeys.ts exports STORAGE_KEYS (with `as const` for literal types) covering all 9 noteser-* keys. Updated 10 consumers: 7 stores, repoStorage.ts (per-repo derived keys still build off STORAGE_KEYS), attachments.ts, page.tsx (migrate path). String values byte-for-byte identical — existing user data unaffected.

items.brief: Tasks metadata milestone (bundled): inline emoji syntax for due / scheduled / start dates and priority markers (⏫🔼🔽⏬). Extends Task type, taskQuery parser, and rendered preview. Foundation for v5j8 (task modal) and v6k9 (recurring tasks). Reference: Obsidian Tasks plugin Emoji Format.
items.result: tasks.ts gains parseTaskMetadata + a TaskPriority union ('highest'|'high'|'normal'|'low'|'lowest') + numeric PRIORITY_WEIGHT. Task interface adds dueDate, scheduledDate, startDate, priority. extractTasks now strips every marker into structured fields. toggleTaskLineText preserves priority + due/scheduled/start markers when (un)checking — only the ✅ done date is added/removed. TaskQueryBlock renders the metadata as inline badges (⏫/🔼/🔽/⏬ for priority, 📅/⏳/🛫/✅ + date for the four date kinds). 11 new tests cover parser semantics, multi-marker round-trip, priority mapping, and toggle-preserves-metadata. 380 total green.

items.brief: Task create/edit modal — dedicated modal (Cmd+Shift+T?) with date pickers, priority dropdown, recurrence input, description field. Writes back the emoji-format syntax to the current line. Builds on v5h7's data model.
items.result: Shipped via parallel agent. New TaskEditModal (Mod+Shift+T) opens for the task line under the cursor. Reads/parses via parseTaskMetadata, populates form: description, status (open/done), due/scheduled/start dates, priority dropdown. Save serializes back via new serializeTaskLine + canonical marker order (priority → 📅 → ⏳ → 🛫 → ✅). 26 new tests (17 serialize + 9 modal render).

items.brief: Recurring tasks — 🔁 every week / 🔁 every month on the 1st syntax. Toggling a recurring task as done creates the next instance one period out and stamps the current one ✅. Builds on v5h7.
items.result: Shipped in commit 3b3bea2 + VS16 fix in 06d0dce. See docs/feature-test-checklist.md.

items.brief: Backlinks panel — sidebar tab listing every note that links to the currently open one (via [[Title]] or unlinked text mentions). In-memory index over notes, useMemo refresh on save. Obsidian's defining feature.
items.result: Shipped via parallel agent. New ribbon nav button (LinkIcon, after Tags) + BacklinksView sidebar panel. New src/utils/backlinks.ts exposes findBacklinks(notes, target) which matches against the target's title AND any aliases (case-insensitive, pipe-display friendly: [[Target|see this]] still matches). Returns up to 3 snippets per linker. Each row clickable to open the linker. 15 new tests cover alias matching, pipe display, deleted-notes excluded, target self-references excluded.

items.brief: Note embeds / transclusion ![[Title]] — render another note's full content inline in both live-preview and rendered-preview. Resolves from the note store at render time; depth-cap for circular embeds.
items.result: Shipped (rendered-preview path): src/utils/embeds.ts (expandEmbeds + extractEmbedTitles, MAX_EMBED_DEPTH=4). Recursive expansion, cycle detection, depth cap, missing-target callout. Wired before renderWikilinks in EditorContent. 13 new tests. Live-preview path not yet wired — rendered preview only for v1.

items.brief: YAML frontmatter properties UI — render --- … --- as a structured key-value table above the note body (date / number / checkbox / list). Must not corrupt the tags: field GitHub sync already round-trips.
items.result: Shipped: frontmatter parser (src/utils/frontmatter.ts) + FrontmatterPanel above editor. 16 tests. b1c3e8a.

items.brief: Note aliases — aliases: [Short Name] frontmatter key lets [[Short Name]] resolve to the note. Extend wikilinks.ts autocomplete + resolver. Small scope.
items.result: Shipped earlier. src/utils/aliases.ts + wikilink resolver updates.

items.brief: Outline / heading navigator — sidebar tab listing H1–H6 headings of the active note, clickable to jump to that line. Parse headings from current note content reactively.
items.result: Shipped via parallel agent. New ribbon nav button (ListBulletIcon, after Calendar) + OutlineView sidebar panel. New src/utils/outline.ts exposes extractHeadings(content) which parses ATX headings (#, ##, …, ######) and ignores anything inside fenced code blocks (handles both ``` and ~~~, and longer-fence > shorter-fence nesting). New SCROLL_TO_LINE_EVENT in src/utils/events.ts; EditorContent listens and dispatches a CodeMirror scrollIntoView + focus when the target matches the active note. 19 new tests cover headings inside fences are skipped, nested fence lengths, no-text-after-# is rejected, indented `#` is rejected, six levels.

items.brief: Task query: extend group by / sort by — group by tag (already have folder/filename/status), sort by due / priority / created. Builds on v5h7.
items.result: Shipped via parallel agent. taskQuery.ts: new filter kinds (due/scheduled before/after/on YYYY-MM-DD, hasDue, noDue, hasScheduled, noScheduled, priorityIs, priorityAbove, priorityBelow); new GroupBy values (tag, priority); new SortKey union (due, priority, created, status, title); ExecutedTask gains noteCreatedAt + tags fields. groupTasks accepts a third arg sortKey and stable-sorts within each group. explainQuery describes everything new. +43 tests (50 → 93 in taskQuery.test.ts).

items.brief: Block-level linking [[Note#^block-id]] — link to a specific paragraph / list item with an auto-generated block ID anchor. Touches sync (block IDs modify note content on link creation).
items.result: Shipped v1 navigation only: [[Note#Heading]] and [[Note#^block-id]] resolve to the target line. Ctrl+Click in editor + click in rendered preview both work. v2: auto-generate block ids on demand (not yet). 19 new tests in wikilinkTarget.test.ts.

items.brief: Replace the auto-sync interval dropdown with a free-form custom-minutes input. Any positive integer is valid; 0 disables. Drops the AutoSyncInterval union type.
items.result: Shipped in a81bcb9.

items.brief: Smart 3-way merge for non-overlapping conflict regions. Currently any divergence (both local + remote changed since lastPushed) fires a manual merge tab. Add: fetch the lastPushed blob from GitHub as the common ancestor, run a proper 3-way merge — if the changes don't overlap (different lines), auto-pick both. Only fall back to the merge editor when there's genuine overlap. Reduces sync friction for the common 'edited two different parts on two devices' case.
items.result: Shipped. New threeWayMerge(ancestor, local, remote) in src/utils/lineDiff.ts returns either {ok:true, merged} or {ok:false, reason:'conflicting-overlap'}. pullFromGitHub fetches the lastPushed blob via getBlobContent and attempts auto-merge before emitting a conflict; on success emits a new 'autoMerged' classification kind ({noteId, remoteSha, mergedContent}). applyNonConflicts handles autoMerged by writing the merged content + bumping gitLastPushedSha=remoteSha so the next push uploads cleanly. formatSyncMessage appends 'auto-merged N' to the status line. 23 new threeWayMerge tests (429 total). The user's Obsidian-induced conflict loop should now auto-resolve when the edits don't touch the same lines.

items.brief: AI settings + client wrapper. Settings UI for BYO API key + provider (Anthropic/OpenAI/off) + model name. New aiClient.ts exposes runPrompt({ system, messages }) that picks the provider, handles browser-direct headers (anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access), and surfaces errors. Foundation for z0e6 + a1f7.
items.result: Shipped (worktree merged into main). settingsStore gains AIProvider type ('off' | 'anthropic' | 'openai'), DEFAULT_AI_MODEL map, aiProvider/aiApiKey/aiModel fields + setters. New src/utils/aiClient.ts exposes runPrompt({ system, messages }) for both providers with the anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access header for Anthropic. New AISection component with three Fields (provider/key/model). SettingsTextInput primitive extended with optional type='text' | 'password' so the API key field can mask. 8 new tests in src/__tests__/aiClient.test.ts cover off-guard, empty-key-guard, both providers' happy paths + system-omitted, and 4xx surfacing. 406 total green.

items.brief: Note-level AI actions. Right-click menu (and command palette once that exists) for: Summarize note, Extract tasks, Suggest tags, Rewrite for clarity, Translate. Uses aiClient.runPrompt with a per-action system prompt; result either replaces selection, inserts at cursor, or opens a comparison modal. Requires y9d5.

Embeddings + semantic search. Index every note via an embedding model (OpenAI text-embedding-3-small or Voyage); store vectors in IDB. New 'find related notes' panel that ranks by cosine similarity to the active note's embedding, and a semantic-search mode in the search modal. Requires y9d5. Re-index on save (debounced).

Configurable keyboard shortcuts. Data-driven SHORTCUTS registry, per-shortcut user overrides persisted in settingsStore, Settings → Shortcuts UI that lets the user click any combo and press a new one to rebind. Cross-platform Mod handling (Ctrl ↔ Meta on Mac).
items.result: Shipped via parallel agent. New src/utils/shortcuts.ts exposes SHORTCUTS registry, parseCombo / matchEvent / formatEventAsCombo / comboToDisplay / activeComboFor helpers, and the ShortcutAction union. settingsStore gains shortcutOverrides + setShortcutOverride / clearShortcutOverride / resetShortcutOverrides. useKeyboardShortcuts refactored from if/else ladder to data-driven loop over SHORTCUTS; subscribes to overrides so changes take effect without a reload. Mac Cmd is collapsed with Ctrl into a single Mod modifier — same defaults work on both platforms. New ShortcutsSection.tsx (Settings) shows every shortcut with a click-to-capture rebind UI, modifier-only events ignored, Escape cancels. 45 new tests.

items.brief: Ctrl+Shift+P command palette (VS Code / Obsidian-style). Fuzzy-search modal listing every command (SHORTCUTS registry + hand-coded extras + active notes as Open commands). User direction 2026-05-19.
items.result: Shipped via parallel agent. New src/utils/commands.ts assembles the command list — every SHORTCUTS entry, hand-coded extras (Open Settings, Sync now/Connect/Disconnect based on state, Toggle preview, Reset all settings, Open templates, etc.), and up to 500 active notes as 'Open: <title>'. New CommandPalette.tsx renders inside the existing Modal with autofocused input + Fuse.js fuzzy search + ↑/↓/Enter navigation. Shortcut registered as openCommandPalette (default Ctrl+Shift+P, rebindable via Settings). 28 new tests.

items.brief: Settings redesign — category navigator on the LEFT (Obsidian-style two-pane layout). Each category (General, Editor, Tasks, GitHub, AI, Shortcuts, About) is its own pane on the right. Replaces the current single-column form. User confirmation via Telegram screenshot 2026-05-19.
items.result: Shipped. New file src/components/modals/SettingsModal.tsx is now a 2-pane layout: left-rail category navigator + right-pane panels. Categories: General, Editor, Attachments, Daily Notes, Templates, GitHub, AI, Shortcuts, Export, About. Modal grew a 2xl/3xl size + bodyless prop. 7 new tests in settingsModal.test.tsx; 40 suites / 763 tests green.

items.brief: Beta features toggle + per-feature flag map in settingsStore. useFlag() hook. Settings UI section. See docs/beta-and-bug-reporting.md for the full design.
items.result: Shipped: betaEnabled + betaFlags in settingsStore, src/utils/featureFlags.ts (FLAGS + useFlag + isFlagOn), new Beta panel in Settings (gated list, master toggle). Example flags: database-view, share-via-url.

items.brief: Report-a-bug modal in Settings → creates a GitHub issue using the existing OAuth token. Diagnostics attached (sanitised), preview before submit, clipboard fallback on error. See docs/beta-and-bug-reporting.md.
items.result: Shipped: src/utils/bugReport.ts (buildIssueBody + sanitizeSettings + createGitHubIssue), src/components/modals/BugReportModal.tsx (form + preview + clipboard fallback). Triggered from Settings → About → Report a bug, plus command palette. Sanitizer strips token/key/secret/password/apikey. 13 new tests in bugReport.test.ts.

items.brief: Database/table view (Bases equivalent) — render notes filtered by frontmatter as an editable table. Highest-impact recommendation from the Reddit research pass (docs/research/obsidian-reddit-insights.md).
items.result: Shipped v1 (read-only): basesQuery.ts (parser + executor for from / where tag / where folder / where property / columns / sort / limit), BasesBlock.tsx (clickable table), basesLivePreview.tsx (CodeMirror inline rendering), ReactMarkdown CodeBlock branch for rendered preview. Inline cell editing deferred to v2. 14 new tests.

items.brief: Periodic Notes — weekly / monthly / quarterly notes baked in alongside daily notes. Community treats these as core, not optional. See research doc.
items.result: Shipped: src/utils/periodicNotes.ts (openThisWeekNote/openThisMonthNote), 4 new settings keys + 2 systemFolder entries, dateFormat tokens WW/W/Q (ISO week + quarter), 2 command-palette entries. 5 new tests in periodicNotes.test.ts. Defaults: Notes/Weekly + YYYY-WW, Notes/Monthly + YYYY-MM.

items.brief: One-click share via secret URL — read-only public link to a note. Leverages Noteser being browser-first; leapfrogs Obsidian on collaboration friction.
items.result: Shipped: hash-fragment share-via-URL. No backend — payload (title + content) encoded as URL-safe base64 in the URL fragment. New /share route decodes + renders read-only markdown. Command palette has Copy share link for current note. Confirm dialog for >8KB notes. 11 new tests.

items.brief: VS Code-style Git UI — source-control sidebar panel showing per-note pending changes (created / modified / deleted) before push. Per-file include/exclude. Diff preview on click. Replaces the blind "Commit & Sync" button.
items.result: Shipped v1: SourceControlPanel.tsx mounted inside GitHubView. Cheap heuristic classifier (gitPath + updatedAt vs lastSyncedAt) so it runs on every store change without burning CPU. Three buckets — created (green +), modified (yellow pencil), deleted (red trash). Click row opens the note. v2 ideas: per-file include/exclude + inline diff preview.

items.brief: Status bar footer — Obsidian-style bottom strip: word + character count of the active note, sync status, current GitHub branch, last-sync relative time.
items.result: Enhanced existing EditorFooter (most of sb7y was already there): added live Syncing… spinner when useGitHubStore.isSyncing, plus a clickable N pending chip that opens the GitHub source-control view. Reuses syncChanges classifier so numbers agree with vsg1.

items.brief: Sync robustness — large-vault paging (>1000 notes), rate-limit handling (429 backoff), partial-failure recovery (resume from last successful blob).
items.result: Shipped: src/utils/githubFetch.ts (retry wrapper around fetch). Honours Retry-After + X-RateLimit-Reset headers, exponential backoff with jitter on 429/5xx, returns non-transient statuses immediately. All 18 fetch sites in github.ts switched to githubFetch. 12 new tests in node-env. Large-vault paging deferred — current truncation handling is acceptable until users hit it.

items.brief: Test coverage for githubSync.ts orchestration — pull/push classification, conflict + autoMerged branches, gitLastPushedSha bookkeeping.
items.result: Shipped: 10 new pullFromGitHub classifier tests covering unchanged / remoteCreated / remoteUpdated / autoMerged / conflict (with + without ancestor) / remoteDeleted / conflictDeleted / mixed batches / non-.md routing. Mocks the github.ts API surface so the orchestration becomes pure-testable. Real threeWayMerge runs inside the mock.

items.brief: Mobile / small-screen responsive layout — collapsible sidebar by default below a width threshold, single-pane mode, touch-friendly tab bar.
Security hardening — XSS surface review in rendered markdown (ReactMarkdown defaults are safe but custom code-block paths may not be), tighter auth on /api/github/* proxy routes.
Guided onboarding — first-run vault picker with starter templates (Zettelkasten, Daily Notes system, Project tracker, Research). Top researcher recommendation for top-of-funnel.
items.result: Shipped: 4 starter vaults (Zettelkasten / Daily Notes system / Project tracker / Research) in src/utils/starterVaults.ts + OnboardingModal triggered on first run (no notes + onboardingShown=false). Each vault seeds 3-7 example notes; first one (README) auto-opens. 11 new tests. Persists dismissal via setOnboardingShown.

items.brief: e4t8 v2 — "Copy block ref" command that auto-mints a ^block-id marker on the current paragraph + copies the [[Note#^id]] link to clipboard.
items.result: Shipped: src/utils/blockRef.ts (generateBlockId/extractTrailingBlockId/appendBlockId/buildBlockRefLink) + command palette entry + CodeMirror event listener. Idempotent: keeps an existing ^id if present, mints one only when missing. 13 new tests.

items.brief: d6v8 v2 — inline cell editing in the Bases table view. Edits write back to the source note frontmatter via writeFrontmatter.
items.result: Shipped: BasesBlock cells are now click-to-edit. Frontmatter fields write back via writeFrontmatter; title field writes to note.title; folder/path/modified/created/tags are read-only. Empty value removes the property. Enter saves, Escape cancels, blur saves.

items.brief: s8h3 v2 — "Import to my vault" CTA on the /share page that creates a copy of the shared note in the recipient's noteStore.
items.result: Shipped: /share page gets an Import to my vault button that redirects to /?import=<fragment>. Main app on mount decodes + prompts confirm + addNote + openNote, then strips the URL param. Same fragment format as s8h3 so the import IS the share payload.
